channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Violence
item.description: Baseline info in general violence page. Intraspecies Concerning our closest living relatives, the great apes, intraspecific killing has frequently been reported across multiple chimpanzee (Pan troglodytes) communities e.g.,5,6,7 and gorilla (Gorilla gorilla)8,9,10 groups. However, it is nearly absent in bonobos (Pan paniscus) (but see11) and orangutans (Pongo ssp.)12. Chimpanzee Rates of intraspecific killings vary considerably among chimpanzee communities, with adult males being both the main attackers and the main victims11. The majority of killings involve intercommunity rather than intracommunity attacks, and most often are made by coalitions of males during territorial boundary patrols5,13.
